chunks.title: video
chunks.text: now as we have assignments, we have another type of operators which are called logical operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: there are only three logical operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: first is NOT

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: second is AND

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and third is OR

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: NOT operator

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: if any value is TRUE in any variable

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: NOT operator converts it to FALSE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and if it is FALSE value

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: then it converts it to TRUE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so NOT operator basically

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: reverses the value

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: for example

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: if I have a variable

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: let's call this variable

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: a simple thing

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: if the value is FALSE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: then we can write NOT of variable

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and now the value which will be printed

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: that will be a TRUE value

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: let's clear this and print

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: now we have TRUE print

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so if we want the opposite of the value of the variable

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: then we will get that

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: for example, if there is no variable here but some expression

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: for example

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: 5 is greater than 8

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: now I know what is 5 is greater than 8

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: this is a FALSE value

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: but what will be the opposite of FALSE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: opposite of FALSE will be TRUE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so here also we will get TRUE print

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so this is how

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: the NOT operator works

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: similarly we have AND operator

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: AND operator basically works

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: to calculate the value on the basis of two operations

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: for example

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: this is our final result

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and this is our value 1

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and this is our value 2

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: value 1 and value 2

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: if both are TRUE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: then our AND operator will also give us TRUE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: but if any of the two is FALSE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: then in that case AND operator will be FALSE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: if this is FALSE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and the other value is TRUE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: then also it will give FALSE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: if both are FALSE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: then also it will return FALSE value

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so AND operator gives TRUE only when

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: both the values of both are TRUE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: for example, let's clear this

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: NOT operator is used with a single value

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and AND operator is used with two expressions

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: for example, here we have

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: one expression

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: 5 is greater than 3

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and the other expression is

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: 3 is greater than 2

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so this is also TRUE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so what should be our final result

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: final result should be equal to TRUE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: we can also verify this is TRUE value

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: but if we write here 3 is greater than 8

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so this is TRUE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and this is FALSE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: as soon as it is FALSE, our final result is also going to be FALSE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so if any of the two AND operator

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: gives FALSE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: then that AND operator also returns FALSE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: OR kind of AND is opposite

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: how does OR operator work

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: let's minimize this

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so let's resize this

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: this is basically our AND operator

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: how does OR operator work

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: we will create the same table for it

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so this is value 1

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: this is value 2

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and this is our result

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: OR operator

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: if both values are TRUE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: then it will return TRUE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: if any of the two values is FALSE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: but the other value is TRUE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: then also it will return TRUE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: if first value is FALSE and second is TRUE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: then also it will return TRUE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: but if both values are FALSE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: then our OR operator will return FALSE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so this is the overall logic of the OR operator

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: OR operator returns FALSE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: OR operator returns FALSE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: when both values are FALSE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: if we write OR instead of AND

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so this is going to give us a TRUE value

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: because this is TRUE and this is FALSE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: but if any of the two is TRUE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: then OR will give us TRUE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: let's clear this and run our code

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: this is going to be TRUE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: but if we make this also FALSE

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: let's make it 13

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: then we are going to get a FALSE value

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so this is how our logical operators work

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: we have studied 4 main types of operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: we have Arithmetic Operators, Relational Operators, Assignment Operators and Logical Operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: apart from this in Python

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: we have Bitwise Operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: plus we also have something called

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Membership Operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: these are the operators which are not really needed right now

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so in the next chapters

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: if we need these operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: then we will study them in detail

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: now to test the knowledge of our operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: we are going to write a very simple code

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and we have to try to predict its output

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so in the code

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so in the code

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: let's suppose we have a variable

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: whose value we have initialized as 3

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and after that we write a line

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: x plus equal to 5

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and here we want to print the value of x

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: we have to predict the output of this value

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: it should be a 2 second, 3 second computation

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: for us and this is going to be very easy

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so by pausing here

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: you can think about it, you can calculate it

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: we have understood the operators very well

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so you just have to understand this line

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: which is very easy

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so our answer is going to be 8

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so we are going to get 8 value

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so operators are very simple

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and we will use operators in almost every class

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so slowly we will use these operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: our practice will also increase
